center.name: 中南半島
center.summary: 中南半島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联37种鸟类，包括白鹇、普通燕鸻、普通夜鹰等37种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to Eastern Himalaya, western and southeastern Indochina, the Malay Peninsula, Bukit Barisan and montane Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-rumped falcon (Neohierax insignis) is a species of bird of prey in the family Falconidae native to Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|right|Fairy pitta in southern Thailand The fairy pitta is a migratory species and travels from Northeast Asia, where it breeds in summer (April–September) to South and Southeast Asia to spend the winter (October–March). Sightings have been reported from India and Indochina, including both the Bruneian and Kalimantan sides of the island of Borneo, and New Guinea, as well as in China, North Korea, South Korea, and Taiwan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The crested myna (Acridotheres cristatellus), also known as the Chinese starling, is a species of starling in the genus Acridotheres native to southeastern China and Indochina.Feare, C., Craig, A., Croucher, B., Shields, C., Komolphalin, K

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in south-eastern Asia from China and India to Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The great hornbill is native to the forests of India, Bhutan, Nepal, Mainland Southeast Asia and Sumatra.{{cite book|author1=Robinson, H.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This fairy-bluebird is found in forests across tropical southern Asia, Indochina and the Greater Sundas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in South-east Asia from the Himalayas to southern China, northwestern Thailand and northern Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in East Asia from northern and eastern India to south-eastern China, Indochina, Hainan, and Sumatra

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|220x220px|Fighting, Madagascar The common myna is native to Asia, with its initial home range spanning Iran, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka, Afghanistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Myanmar, Malaysia, Singapore, peninsular Thailand, Indochina, Japan (both mainland Japan and the Ryukyu Islands) and China. thumb|left|A common myna as an invasive species in Gülhane Park, Turkey thumb|Visiting a home garden in Colombo, [[Sri Lanka]]The common myna has been introduced to many other parts of the world such as Canada, Australia, Turkey, Israel, New Zealand, New Caledonia, Fiji, the United States (South Florida only), South Africa, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Cayman Islands, Islands in the Indian Ocean (the Seychelles – from which it was subsequently eradicated at great expense,{{Cite journal| journal= PLOS ONE|first1=Thomas|last1=Evans|first2=Elena|last2=Angulo|first3=Corey J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in China and winters in Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in dense wet forests in the Himalayas east through southwestern China to northern Indochina, and with disjunct populations (but possibly separate species) on Iriomote Island off southern Japan, and on Sumatra and Java in Indonesia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Types of the Eurylaimidae|url=https://www.aviansystematics.org/uploads/downloads/103/file/ZV331_077-088.pdf|journal=Zoologische Verhandelingen|volume=331|pages=77–88 [80] |access-date=16 April 2022|archive-date=11 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210511015956/https://www.aviansystematics.org/uploads/downloads/103/file/ZV331_077-088.pdf|url-status=live}} }} The () is a species of bird in the typical broadbill family Eurylaimidae found in Mainland Southeast Asia and the Greater Sunda Islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The grey nightjar (Caprimulgus jotaka; also "gray" in some non-local literature) is a species of nightjar found in East Asia, breeding from southeastern Russia south through China, the Korean Peninsula and Japan to northern Indochina and westward along the Himalayas. It is largely migratory, wintering in Indochina south to Java in Indonesia, but is resident in warmer areas in the south of its breeding range. It is sometimes treated as a subspecies of the jungle nightjar (C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Oriental pratincole is native to Asia, breeding from North Pakistan and the Kashmir region, and sporadically southwards towards the Maldives and Sri Lanka, Indochina, eastern China, Manchuria and the Philippines

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rusty-naped pitta is native to Indochina and adjacent parts of southern China

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the Himalayas, Purvanchal Range and southern China ; some birds winter to northern Indochina

It is found in southeastern Asia from the Himalayas through Indochina to southwestern Cambodia

birds.evidence: It is native to southern China, Hainan and Indochina

birds.label: 分布于

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Paradoxornis gularis |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22735202A264185791 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22735202A264185791.en |access-date=12 June 2025}} | genus = Paradoxornis | species = gularis | authority = Gray, GR, 1845 | synonyms = * Psittiparus gularis }} The grey-headed parrotbill (Paradoxornis gularis) is a parrotbill in the family Sylviidae and is found in eastern Asia from the Himalayas to Indochina and Hainan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the limestone hills of northern Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Its range extends from the Himalayas through southern China and Indochina (the Cardamom and Annamite ranges in particular)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the Himalayas, centre China, Yunnan and northern Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Birds from various origin sites may end up in the Middle East (mainly Arabia), South Asia (from Pakistan, most often Punjab and Sind, northern India, and Nepal), east to Indochina, the Malay Peninsula, and southern and eastern China. Greater spotted eagles on an Eastern European track predominantly migrate to the Middle East or Northeast Africa, while others migrate through the Carpathian Mountains to the Balkan Peninsula, and some continue through Central Europe and Western Europe to Southwestern Europe. The main wintering sites of the Asian populations are located in the Arabian Peninsula, Indian subcontinent, Indochina Peninsula, and East China. During migration, greater spotted eagles commonly cover around per day but can cover up to within a day.Hedenström, A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native and breeds in Manchuria, the Russian Far East and neighboring areas; it winters in Indochina and southern China. The birds are occasionally seen in Japan. They have also been observed in Hong Kong and Singapore. The bird's population is unknown, but is not increasing or declining. The white-throated rock thrush is said to be rare in North Korea. However, it is relatively common in China, except on the coast. The bird's IUCN Red List category is "least concern". The white-throated rock thrush inhabits forested areas, shrubland and rocky areas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Note the brown patches, typical of sub-adult males]] The silver pheasant (Lophura nycthemera) is a species of pheasant found in forests, mainly in mountains, of mainland Southeast Asia and eastern and southern China

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This bird occurs in southern Burma (Myanmar), Thailand, Indo-China, Sumatra, Java and Bali in scrub and secondary growth, open fields and gardens, bamboo thickets and open forest often near villages

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|A wreathed hornbill in flight The wreathed hornbill inhabits tropical evergreen forests in the region from southern Bhutan, Northeast India, Bangladesh and through mainland Southeast Asia to Indonesia, where it is restricted to Sumatra, Java, Borneo and a few smaller islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in central China and the Himalayas (where it winters in the southern foothills, as well as in southern Yunnan, the Patkai and northern Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species is migratory, breeding in the mountains of north-east China and migrating to subtropical or tropical moist montane forest in India, and Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Paradoxornis bakeri |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22735612A264210558 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22735612A264210558.en |access-date=12 June 2025}} | genus = Paradoxornis | species = bakeri | authority = (Hartert, 1900) | synonyms = * Psittiparus bakeri }} The rufous-headed parrotbill (Paradoxornis bakeri), or greater rufous-headed parrotbill, is a parrotbill in the family Paradoxornithidae and is found in eastern Asia from the eastern Himalayas to Indochina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is strongly migratory and winters mainly in subtropical south China, northern Thailand and elsewhere in northeastern Indochina. It is rare but annual in Japan. Pallas's leaf warbler breeds in coniferous taiga forests including fir, spruce, pine and larch, or in mixed forest with willow, alder, and a high percentage of conifers
